title: Design QT Stock Detail page rebuild with ui-ux-pro-max
decisions: chose full rebuild-from-scratch of the Stock Detail page over patching the existing UI because the user judged visualization quality the product differentiator and the current page 'looks shitty'
outcome: done — full design spec produced (tokens + 15-component build list), implementation not in this thread
summary: User demanded a from-scratch rebuild of the QT Stock Detail page using the ui-ux-pro-max skill. Claude queried the skill's design database and produced a complete design system: dark tokens (--bg-primary #0a0e17, --accent #00ff88, --negative #ff5555, Inter with tabular-nums) plus a 15-component plan (StockHeader, PriceChart via lightweight-charts, OptionChainPanel fed by sled-dhan-web /optchain, ForecastPanel/MFHoldingsTable/InstitutionalFlow fed by sled-scanx). Page layout uses 5 tabs: Chart, Options, Financials, Ownership, Activity, orchestrated by StockDetail.tsx.

title: Polish FundamentalsPanel to TradingView quality and weigh PWA vs extension
decisions: chose a hybrid distribution (thin extension as cookie bridge + PWA app) over pure PWA because a PWA cannot read enctoken from kite.zerodha.com or ci_session from chartink.com cookies
chose launching a dedicated agent for the 2000+ line FundamentalsPanel rewrite over inline editing because of the rewrite's size
outcome: done — rewritten FundamentalsPanel builds and is live; hybrid PWA+extension direction agreed
summary: User compared the extension UI to TradingView and called it hacky. Claude used ui-ux-pro-max to diagnose 5 gaps (flat SVG charts without gradient fills, no 24/32/48px spacing rhythm, weak typography hierarchy, undersized charts, no elevation), then an agent fully rewrote FundamentalsPanel in QT-chrome-extension with gradient fills, bezier curves, donut glows and gauge arcs; npm build passed. The thread then analyzed PWA vs Chrome extension distribution, concluding the PWA's blocker is no chrome.cookies access to Chartink/Zerodha sessions, leading to a hybrid cookie-bridge-extension + PWA proposal.

title: Consolidate 6 SLED audits into critical gap list
outcome: done — comprehensive gap list produced (13 CRITICAL + HIGH items)
summary: The 'Audit sled-db' background agent completed (verified all 67 trait methods across UserStore/ObservabilityStore/TelemetryStore/CollectorStore implemented in both SQLite and Postgres, 32 tables matching models), and Claude consolidated all 6 SLED audits into a gap list. CRITICAL findings: sled-dl-feed maps 4 of 7 tick packet types to wrong byte codes and reads prev_oi via float cast; sled-collector drops OpenInterest (0x25) entirely; sled-insight returns 0 max-drawdown when all trades lose; sled-db Postgres reads TIMESTAMPTZ as String and binds bools as i64 (runtime panics), and release_slice has no HTTP route. HIGH items include NaN partial_cmp().unwrap() panics, unenforced watchlist symbol limits, and lost WS subscriptions after reconnect.

title: Design backfill pipeline: PID-keyed candles schema and token relay
decisions: chose fresh re-collection with PID v3 as primary key over backfilling PIDs into existing TimescaleDB candles because PID v3 is incompatible with v1 and existing data has gaps and NULL PIDs

chose a 3-tier token relay (manual admin paste page now, admin extension soon, full admin UI later) all hitting the same POST /admin/auth/{broker} endpoint, over server-side headless browser automation (fragile)
chose building sled-backfill before sled-backfill-store, with the instrument universe (DHAN CSV -> PID v3 -> instrument table) unlocked first
kept Chartink out of historical candle sources — only sled-scanx (openweb-ticks), sled-dhan-web, and sled-zerodha-web, all already normalizing to qt_core::market::Candle
outcome: design decided; sled-server-db manifest writing was interrupted by the user mid-thread
summary: Designed the historical-candle backfill pipeline for qt-app. Compared raw candle formats (DHAN column arrays, Zerodha row arrays with +0530 TZ quirk, openweb-ticks delivery_pct flag, Chartink MarkedCandle) and confirmed all broker SLEDs already normalize to qt_core::market::Candle. Finalized a TimescaleDB candles hypertable keyed on (pid_underlying, pid_contract, timeframe, ts) with nullable oi/delivery_pct plus source and confidence columns. Decided fresh re-collection over PID backfill, a tiered admin token-relay via POST /admin/auth/{broker}, and sled-backfill before sled-backfill-store; ended writing the sled-server-db (TimescaleDB+Redis) manifest when the user interrupted.

title: Consolidate PWA-to-sidecar migration audit across 4 agents
outcome: done — 52-call audit consolidated into a phased migration plan
summary: Four parallel audit agents mapped the PWA (React 19, Vite 6, Zustand 5, lightweight-charts) ahead of migrating from the Node server (:3000) to the Rust sidecar (:4100). Found 52 unique API calls across 3 servers (Node 38, ScanX :4002 6 ISIN-based, DHAN :4003 7 sec_id-based); only 6 endpoints match the sidecar directly, 19 (backtest, watchlists, portfolio, auth/cookies) have no equivalent, plus identifier mismatches (ISIN vs symbol, dhanSecId vs symbol) and a bulk /api/master/instruments map the sidecar lacks. Also flagged 9 scattered base-URL constants, a hardcoded localhost:3000 in PriceSection.tsx, 50 hardcoded Zerodha tokens in zerodha-ws.ts, and that PID fields are already wired in stores but unused; Phase 1 is API-client consolidation.

title: Reverse-engineer Chartink groupavg and GROUP BY sector widget queries
outcome: done — 23 patterns documented in the dashboard doc
summary: User pasted captured curls to chartink.com/widget/process; Claude URL-decoded the query payloads with Python and appended new patterns to arena/docs/CHARTINK-DASHBOARD.md, reaching 23 documented patterns. Key discoveries: groupavg() for universe-wide metric averages, GROUP BY sector for one-call sector rotation tables, the not() operator for universe exclusion ({45603} not(condition)), [=N] session-candle offsets, FnO lot size usable in arithmetic for server-side P&L, and '1 month ago' lookback for monthly pivots. Declared the /widget/process SQL dialect thoroughly mapped and ready for the extension build planning.

title: Decode Chartink intraday offsets and breadth oscillator widgets
outcome: done — 17 patterns documented
summary: Another batch of captured chartink.com/widget/process curls (Mswing breadth widget, WMA/ADX scanner) decoded and documented into arena/docs/CHARTINK-DASHBOARD.md, totaling 17 patterns. Discoveries: intraday candle offsets [0],[-1]..[-4] on 5-minute candles (distinct from [=1]/[=-1] in scan_clause), a manual SMA of groupcount giving a server-side smoothed breadth oscillator, the groupcount(impossible)+80 trick for constant threshold reference lines, ORDER BY ascending support, and confirmation that WMA/ADX/EMA/SMA all work with the 'latest' prefix in widget SELECTs.

title: Wire 37 Tauri IPC commands — blocked by full disk
outcome: blocked — ENOSPC (451GB/494GB used) prevented all file writes; code delivered as text, cargo clean advised
summary: The 'Wire Tauri IPC commands' agent designed a full src-tauri/src/commands.rs replacement: 37 #[tauri::command] functions (health, auth_status, preferences, app state, watchlists, backtests, journal, credits) backed by Arc<SqliteStore> with sled_user validation, plus explicit IPC stubs for candles/portfolio/prices/run_backtest pending ConnectionManager and broker SLEDs. Every write failed with ENOSPC since the disk was at capacity, so the agent emitted the complete implementation as text and prescribed cargo clean in qt-app/target to free 5-20GB before re-applying.

title: Specify qt-core SLED traits for a zero-context coding agent
decisions: chose a new qt-core/src/traits.rs replacing the minimal Sled trait in orchestrator.rs, with manifest-driven capability discovery via SledManifest
outcome: done — exhaustive implementable spec delivered in chat (not written to files)
summary: User asked for implementable qt-core trait details consumable by a zero-context coding agent. Claude grepped real types across qt-core, sled-simulate and sled-portfolio, then wrote an exhaustive spec: new type files (manifest.rs with SledManifest/TraitDeclaration/MethodDeclaration/HealthStatus, auth_types.rs with Credentials/AuthResult/AuthStatus, feed_types.rs, screener_types.rs) and async traits — SledHealth (all 19 SLEDs, id/manifest/health/is_available), BrokerAuth (dhan/zerodha/chartink, with DHAN auth-over-jwttoken priority quirk), DataProvider (candles/day_ohlc/quote/expiries/option_chain with PID resolved internally via Arc<InstrumentTable>), and TickFeed (per-SLED independent WebSockets), each with implementation notes and error conventions on QtError.

title: Discover Chartink count() streak filter and DII/FII holding formulas
outcome: done — 4 patterns added (12 total documented at that point)
summary: First of the May-29 Chartink curl-decoding batches: doji scanner, buyer-vs-seller initiated trades (Bi-Si), and DII holding-trend widgets decoded into arena/docs/CHARTINK-DASHBOARD.md. Major finds: count(N, 1 where condition) as a server-side consecutive-streak counter (e.g. DII increased 4 straight quarters), mixed timeframes in one query (SELECT on 5-min, WHERE on 15-min), reusable custom indicators #52657 (DII) and #52652 (FII) summing 10+ shareholding sub-category percentages, and the Quarterly prefix working in /widget/process — confirming it as Chartink's most permissive parser.

title: Audit sled-dhan-web for boundary, precision, and auth-handling bugs
outcome: done — agent audit report delivered with 4 CRITICAL, 6 HIGH, 7 MEDIUM findings
summary: Background agent audited qt-app's sled-dhan-web. Criticals: WS endpoints return internal SymbolSnapshot/TickEvent instead of qt_core::market::TickSnapshot; tick prices stored as f32 (paisa precision loss on 130,000+ INR stocks like MRF); JULIAN_EPOCH_SECS=315532200 is 600 seconds off, shifting all candle timestamps 10 minutes; serde_json::to_value().unwrap() can panic in 4 handlers. Highs include txn endpoint paths hardcoded instead of config-driven, missing 401/403 QtError::auth_expired checks in expiries/orders/positions, Option<u32> volume/oi overflow risk vs i64 in qt_core, and a fixed 5-second WS reconnect loop that ignores the existing BackoffConfig::websocket_connection().

title: Inventory the Chartink integration across all qt-app subsystems
outcome: done — full Chartink integration audit delivered
summary: Background agent mapped the entire Chartink integration: ~35 files across 5 subsystems (Node server, Chrome connector extension, @qt/core TypeScript, Rust crates, React PWA). Documented all 4 Chartink endpoints with request/response shapes — /screener/process (scans), /backtest/process (scanlink hashes), /oapi (OHLCV with filternumber markers, 200 candles/page pagination), /widget/process (indices/breadth/RRG). Captured key constants (NULL_SENTINEL=1.7e+308, OAPI_PAGE_SIZE=200), the cookie-harvester/CSRF meta-token flow, the connector's XMLHttpRequest interception of scan_clause, and the scan_clause DSL parser pipeline (tokenizer, recursive-descent scan-ast, decomposer).

title: Document the @qt/core TypeScript backtest engine end to end
outcome: done — exhaustive architecture documentation of @qt/core delivered
summary: Background agent read every source file in @qt/core and documented the zero-dependency ESM backtest engine: simulate()/simulateMTF() 4-phase timestamp-batched loop (exits, marker-gated entries, equity recording, portfolio stops), 3 entry rules (next-candle-open default), 5 exit rules (stop-loss, target, two-mode trailing-stop, fixed-period, scan-exit), 4 position sizers (equal-weight, risk-based, fixed-amount, half-Kelly capped at 25%). Covered the Portfolio slippage (0.1% both legs) and dual brokerage model, 23 report metrics (RISK_FREE_RATE_ANNUAL=0.07, 252 trading days), and report modules including JdK RRG and squarified treemap.

title: Fix sled-simulate report metrics to reach parity with the TypeScript engine
decisions: chose to align Rust report.rs to TS conventions (sample variance Sharpe, simple-division daily risk-free, Sortino=99 when no downside, trading-days CAGR, negative drawdown/largest_loss signs, profit-factor cap 99) because core/simulator TS is the reference implementation
outcome: done — 8 metric discrepancies fixed, 56 tests pass, committed and pushed; parity declared CLEAN
summary: Parity audit of sled-simulate (Rust) against core/simulator (TS) found the engine, MTF timeline, all 5 exit rules, 3 entry rules, 4 position sizers, and Portfolio math matching, but 8 significant report-metric discrepancies: Sharpe population-vs-sample variance, compound-vs-simple daily risk-free rate, Sortino edge case (Rust 0 vs TS 99), CAGR on calendar-days/365.25 vs trading-days basis, drawdown and largest_loss sign conventions, profit-factor cap, and MTF portfolio-stop MAE/MFE hardcoded to 0. All report fixes were applied to sled-simulate/src/report.rs to match TS, tests updated, and the change committed.

title: Decide qt-resolver extraction with a strict no-direct-DB rule
decisions: chose resolver-routed DB access over qt-instruments/sync.rs using PgPool directly because the user mandated 'no direct to db at any cost — EVERYTHING GOES THROUGH THE RESOLVER/ROUTER'
chose to build qt-resolver as a separate shared crate extracted from sidecar/src/resolver.rs + data-access/src/resolver.rs + sidecar/src/manifest.rs rather than greenfield
edge designed as a subset of server capability — one resolver design with a pluggable Transport (IPC edge / HTTP server)
chose migrate-not-drop for 84.5M v1 candle rows (1m history irreplaceable)
outcome: compacted mid-design — qt-resolver spec-first extraction queued next, then sled-server-db
summary: Continuation of the living-docs/PID-v3 session: qt-pid (45 tests) and qt-instruments (121K instruments end-to-end against live DB) were built spec-first with the 7-step validation gate. This thread's live work was deciding how to build qt-resolver: surveyed existing assets (two resolvers, CapabilityRegistry in sidecar/src/manifest.rs, no Transport trait found) and the user corrected the plan — qt-instruments' direct PgPool use is an architecture violation that must route resolver -> sled-server-db. Session hit /compact while surveying the sidecar for the extraction.

title: Wire live Zerodha prices everywhere and rebuild the broken RRG chart
decisions: chose to port the original extension's SectorRRG (QT-chrome-extension TearSheet.tsx lines 1133-1800) as a standalone RRGChart component instead of further patching, per user 'STOP PATCHING — rebuild it or use the original'
chose chrome.cookies.get() by name over getAll({domain}) because getAll misses cookies like enctoken
outcome: compacted mid-work — RRG still not rendering; live prices only fully wired on Watchlist
summary: qt-app PWA session: fixed all sled-scanx parsers (forecast needs 'Data' capital-D, MF holdings is array-of-arrays via mfpastholdingsbyisin, FII/DII is {DII:[],FII:[]} across 5 segments), built PriceContext with a single Zerodha WS connection plus useSubscribe ref-counting, 5 Zustand stores, and PriceCell flash animations. RRGChart remained persistently broken after cascading fixes (StrictMode Set init, flat-array response, wrong fetch origin), so the plan shifted to porting the original extension SectorRRG. Compacted while wiring RRG into Dashboard/TearSheet and live prices to all pages.

title: Audit sled-zerodha-web for boundary types, QtError, and WS lifecycle gaps
outcome: done — agent audit delivered (3 CRITICAL, 5 HIGH, 7 MEDIUM, plus LOW findings)
summary: Background agent audited sled-zerodha-web. Criticals: /ws/snapshot endpoints leak the internal ws_client::Tick type instead of qt_core::market::TickSnapshot (instrument_token:i32 vs symbol_id:u32, Depth vs MarketDepth, multiple field/optionality mismatches); all errors are Result<T,String> never using QtError; every handler returns HTTP 200 even on errors. Highs: wss://ws.zerodha.com and api_key 'kitefront' hardcoded ignoring config.yaml, unused qt-pid dependency, unsubscribe() never sends the WS unsubscribe message, reconnect reuses a stale enctoken URL forever, and the 3 req/s rate limiter is hardcoded rather than read from limits.historical_rate.

title: Wire 11 SLEDs over IPC, ship PID v3, and spec sled-server-db
decisions: chose YAML manifests over Rust traits for SLED capability declaration because they are DB-stored, server-pushed, and hot-reloadable
chose PID v3 split (u128 underlying + u64 bit-packed contract) over single u128 because 25-char option symbols overflow 39 digits; rejected hash-based PID for reversibility when trading is enabled
kept exchange codes 51/52/53 from v2 (rejected 40/41/42); replaced hand-rolled date math with chrono::NaiveDate after a roundtrip bug
frontend adapts to backend Rust types, never the reverse ('frontend is dumb renderer')
outcome: compacted — IPC dispatch, PID v3 (38/38 tests), sled-portfolio (147 tests), and docs done; sled-server-db manifest write failed at context limit, spec saved to memory
summary: Three-phase QT session: wired all 11 SLEDs via IPC in the Tauri app 1:1 with HTTP routes (fixing response wrapping, snake_case configs, DHAN auth priority and /getDataH daily candles), built sled-portfolio (17 indicators, signals, scans, correlation, risk), and implemented PID v3 with base-54 encoding and a manifest system (10 built + 8 planned YAML manifests, 131 methods). Documented ADRs 015-018, SLED-GUIDE (16 rules), SLED-CATALOG (19 SLEDs), and the 10-week build plan. Verified API limits: openweb-ticks 200 concurrent no-auth, Zerodha ~10 concurrent/59-day 1m, DHAN 30 concurrent.

title: Audit sled-zerodha-web, sled-dhan-web, and sled-scanx honestly
outcome: done — cross-SLED honest assessment delivered (~30 findings across 3 crates)
summary: Background agent audited all three broker SLEDs. sled-zerodha-web: RwLock .unwrap() poison risk in AuthStore, no WS reconnect loop (task exits permanently on disconnect), i32 volume/oi overflow, stale '2025-01-01' default from-date, full HashMap clone per snapshot request. sled-dhan-web: holdings/orders/positions/margins modules never wired into the main.rs router and userdata_json never stored for them, DHN1804 broker code hardcoded in 3 places, unconditional +19800s IST offset in from_iso, 640KB unused BinaryParser buffer pool, reconnect mutex deadlock, a size_of test asserting 104 bytes that must fail. sled-scanx: config.rs entirely dead (port 4002 and all URLs hardcoded), dead local Candle/StockSnapshot types shadowing qt_core, no HTTP timeout.

title: Debug DHAN WS OI parsing live and land the cross-SLED audit fixes
decisions: chose to verify the OI fix via live snapshot values rather than continue chasing the missing eprintln/println output, since debug prints never appeared despite data demonstrably flowing
outcome: mostly done — OI fix live-verified against DHAN UI; prev_oi (stale-binary/cargo cache issue) and LTT decoding deferred to next session
summary: 185-turn live debugging session on sled-dhan-web's WebSocket for CRUDEOIL (sec_id 499095): fixed OI type-37 packet parsing to read_f32-then-cast-u32, verified live (OI went from 0 to 12,430 vs DHAN UI 12,472); prev_oi type-32 fix is correct in source (1177259008 raw u32 = 10979.0 as f32, plausibly yesterday's closing OI) but the running binary never reflected it — debug eprintln/println never appeared despite snapshots updating, concluded a cargo incremental/stale-binary issue. Also landed the session's audit fixes: serde unwrap panics, f32->f64 tick pipeline, sled-scanx config wiring, sled-zerodha-web QtError with proper HTTP status codes, and extension/server cookie merge-not-replace with USERDATA capture. Final field-by-field snapshot matched DHAN UI on all prices/OHLC/circuits/volume; only prev_oi and last-trade-time remained wrong.

title: Decode Chartink dashboard widget curls into query patterns 26-29
outcome: done — 29 patterns now documented in CHARTINK-DASHBOARD.md
summary: User pasted raw /widget/process curls from Chartink premium dashboards; the assistant URL-decoded them in Python and extracted four new query patterns: a 1-minute volume spike detector using sma([0] 1 minute volume, 500)*10 with 8Cr turnover floor, RSI overbought reversal (1 day ago rsi(14) > 80 and close < 1 day ago low), a min(5, daily_return) >= 0.02 consecutive-gain streak filter, and an RSI oversold bounce mirror. Confirmed Latest Rsi(14) works in SELECT and 1-minute data is available to premium users. Findings were appended as Patterns 26-29 to arena/docs/CHARTINK-DASHBOARD.md, completing the dashboard SQL reference.

title: Audit PWA API calls against sidecar routes for migration
outcome: done — full migration map delivered: 3 READY, ~42 PARTIAL, 7 MISSING endpoints
summary: Background agent audited ~52 PWA fetch() call sites against the sidecar (port 4100). Core finding: a universal /api/ prefix mismatch — api.ts calls VITE_API_URL/api/... while sidecar routes have no prefix; only telemetry.ts already targets the sidecar correctly. Seven endpoints are entirely missing from the sidecar (/api/resample, /api/cookies/zerodha, /api/cookies, /api/heartbeat, backtest progress/queue, GET /watchlists/{id}), POST /backtest has a semantic mismatch (Chartink scan vs Arena config), and dhan-api.ts/scanx-api.ts bypass the sidecar to hit sled ports 4003/4002 directly. ZerodhaWs (wss://ws.zerodha.com) and the DHAN SSE tick stream cannot be proxied by the sidecar.

title: Blind-critic the backfill.xml BackfillJobStore spec for completeness
outcome: done — verdict: spec not complete enough for a blind builder; 5 blockers found
summary: A blind-critic agent re-ran against the backfill.xml spec for BackfillJobStore (enqueue/claim_jobs over the 003_backfill.sql table) without reading backfill.rs. It found 5 BLOCKER-level forks: which provider survives ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, whether enqueue returns rows_affected() vs a client-side distinct count for intra-batch duplicates, undefined u64->i64 bigint binding for pid_contract above i64::MAX, whole-call failure semantics when a claimed row fails decode after the UPDATE commits, and Ok(vec![]) vs error for an empty claim. Majors included unclamped lease_secs/limit, SKIP LOCKED short-batch semantics, and attempts overflowing SMALLINT at 32767 since no executor sets terminal status.

title: Audit sled-scanx for dead config, boundary breaks, and silent errors
outcome: done — audit report with 3 CRITICAL, 6 HIGH, 6 MEDIUM, 6 LOW findings
summary: Background agent audited the sled-scanx crate. Criticals: AppConfig/config.yaml are entirely dead code (ScanXClient::new() hardcodes all URLs), 6 of 13 config.yaml endpoint paths are wrong vs the real ScanX API (e.g. /staticscanx/fiidii vs /staticscanx/fiidiidata), and handle_delivery returns raw serde_json::Value bypassing qt_core::scanx::DeliveryEntry. Highs include the fiidii.rs zero-value key-fallback bug (0.0 treated as missing), no retry/rate-limit handling in client.rs, missing User-Agent/Referer headers, hardcoded token_id in ipo.rs, and fetch_all_fiidii swallowing segment errors via unwrap_or_default(). SLED import rules pass; the qt_core type boundary fails on /delivery.

title: Trace the qt-app server backtest engine end to end
outcome: done — complete end-to-end data-flow audit delivered
summary: Background agent traced the full server-side backtest engine: POST /api/backtest enters a serial FIFO in-memory queue (backtestQueue, runningAbort AbortController), cookies come from a Puppeteer bootstrap harvest plus the QT connector extension, and orchestrator.js runBacktest() runs a 7-step pipeline ending in a worker-thread simulate()/simulateMTF(). The critical mechanism is Chartink's filternumber(scanlink) in /oapi queries, which marks every historical candle where the scan matched (30-way concurrency, 200 candles/page); scan clauses are never evaluated locally. Results persist to the backtest_results table in TimescaleDB (qt_marketdata at localhost:5436) with JSONB config/summary/result columns and a pending->running->completed lifecycle.

title: Audit Tauri IPC plumbing coverage across all PWA endpoints
outcome: done — 42 endpoints audited: 0% fully wired, 71% PARTIAL, 29% MISSING
summary: Background agent audited whether every PWA fetch() has Tauri IPC plumbing (Tauri command + qt-api.ts fn + ROUTE_MAP + Resolver). Result: 30 of 42 endpoints have complete back-end plumbing but 0% are wired end-to-end because components (App.tsx BacktestHistory, NewBacktest.tsx, PriceContext.tsx, LivePanel.tsx, etc.) call fetch() directly instead of qt-api.ts. 12 endpoints have no Tauri command at all — /api/resample, /api/cookies/zerodha, the three /telemetry routes, and all six scanx-api routes plus dhan /expiries and /optchain — even though Resolver methods mostly exist. Eight components/stock/* files import dhanApi/scanxApi directly, and neither ZerodhaWs nor the DHAN EventSource tick stream has any IPC-event equivalent.

title: Continue QT Chrome Extension build: Chartink backtester polish
decisions: chose hidden-DOM-element polling over CustomEvent because MV3 MAIN/ISOLATED worlds don't share events
chose NIFTYBEES over NIFTY as benchmark because NIFTY doesn't exist on /oapi
chose calendar-month RRG windows over trade-count windows because intraday tails were jittery
chose inline service-worker simulation over child Worker because MV3 can't spawn Workers
outcome: ongoing — RRG spline tails being polished; short-direction 0.0 bug still pending
summary: Compaction-continued session of the QT Chrome Extension that turns Chartink scans into a zero-backend TypeScript backtester (intercept query via MAIN-world XHR patch, fan out /oapi with filternumber(scanlink), simulate, render tear sheets). The summary catalogs the full build: 1,857-line CHARTINK-API.md, tokenizer->AST->decomposer MTF pipeline validated 7/7 against real Chartink, 2436-line TearSheet.tsx with Sector RRG, plus many fixed bugs (worker stub scandal, double-space query reconstruction, UTCTimestamp intraday equity curves). Current work was smoothing RRG tails with Catmull-Rom splines and a sector visibility filter; pending items include the short-direction 0.0 bug and landing-page/demo-mode distribution work.

title: Design the MTF query decomposition engine via brainstorming skill
decisions: chose comparing actual cross-TF values by proxying higher-TF values onto the lower-TF timeline over scanlink-only markers
chose cascade universe reduction (each TF filters universe down) to bound /oapi fan-out
chose /screener/process with column_clause to fetch actual per-TF values from Chartink
outcome: done — design approved and fed into the writing-plans/implementation cycle
summary: Brainstorming session designing MTF query decomposition for the QT Chrome Extension: Chartink scans mixing daily/5-min/weekly conditions under AND/OR must be split into per-timeframe condition groups. The assistant decomposed a real example (daily close <= 0.9% * yearly high AND Xpress flag_roce AND 5-minute volume vs sma) into a cascade — daily conditions filter the full universe to U1, 5-min conditions filter U1 to U2, then 5-min OHLCV is fetched for U2 and simulated at 5-min resolution. Key clarifications resolved via AskUserQuestion: cross-TF comparisons proxy higher-TF values onto the lower-TF timeline, and actual values are obtainable via /screener/process column_clause rather than scanlink markers alone.

title: Run round-3 audit of sled-dhan-web tick feed and fixes
outcome: done — recent fixes verified correct; 1 new CRITICAL and 2 HIGH found
summary: Round-3 background audit of sled-dhan-web verified recent fixes: the margins.rs 401/403 -> QtError::auth_expired check matches all 7 endpoints, and the last_trade_time chain (binary u32 -> julian_to_unix u64 -> TickEvent -> SymbolSnapshot -> NaiveDateTime) is complete. New CRITICAL C-1: bid_price/ask_price from TopBidAsk packets are silently dropped at to_tick_snapshot() because qt_core::market::TickSnapshot has no such fields. HIGHs: last_trade_time is UTC while candle timestamps are IST (timestamp.rs adds IST_OFFSET_SECS), and index_value never reaches TickSnapshot so indices show ltp=0.0. Mediums include hardcoded DHN1804 broker code ignoring config and four main.rs handlers returning 500 instead of the QtError status code.

title: Execute 8-task MTF backtest engine plan with subagents
decisions: chose subagent-driven-development with sonnet implementers over manual execution per the superpowers skill flow
enforced no TODOs/stubs/shortcuts and single file per responsibility per user mandate
outcome: done — all 8 tasks complete, 302/302 tests, 0 TS errors, pushed
summary: 98-turn execution of docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-30-mtf-backtest-engine.md using the subagent-driven-development skill: fresh subagent per task with verification after each. Built timeframe-parser.ts (rank TFs, identify signal/scanlink TF), MTFStockData types, mtf-timeline.ts (unified cross-TF timeline), simulateMTF() in engine.ts (entries only fire on signal-TF candles with higherTFConfirmed === true), popup/orchestrator wiring, IndexedDB Map serialization, and 5 integration tests proving no look-ahead bias. Final verification: 302/302 tests, clean build, 0 TypeScript errors across 106 source files and 153 commits, then git push.
